capabilities: Implement a systematic approach to analyze query logs for extracting dataset usage patterns and consumer behavior insights.

procedure.action: Extract table references from query logs by parsing SQL statements using a SQL parser library; store results in a structured format for further analysis using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Aggregate usage data by counting occurrences of each table reference in the parsed logs; use a database or in-memory data structure to maintain counts using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.
Extract popular datasets by selecting tables with usage counts exceeding a predefined threshold, such as the top 10% of all tables by usage frequency.

List consumers by extracting unique user identifiers from query logs; correlate these with dataset usage to determine consumer access patterns using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Analyze usage trends by applying time-series analysis on aggregated usage data; use statistical methods to detect significant changes or patterns over time using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

summary: Track dataset usage and consumer analytics in data catalogs by processing query logs to identify popular datasets and usage trends.
title: Dataset Usage

failureConditions: Inaccurate usage statistics when query logs are incomplete or improperly parsed, causing misleading analytics and decision-making.
Consumer behavior misinterpretation when user identifiers are not consistently logged, leading to incorrect attribution of dataset access.
